headline: COCKROACH PARTY SWARMS TO 22.8 MILLION FOLLOWERS, TAKES ON CHIEF JUSTICE'S 'PARASITE' REMARKS
summary: The Cockroach Janta Party, a satirical Indian youth-based political movement, was founded on May 16, 2026, in response to remarks made by Chief Justice of India Surya Kant, who compared some activists and unemployed youth to 'cockroaches' and 'parasites of society'. The movement, led by Abhijeet Dipke, has gained rapid popularity online, with over 22.8 million Instagram followers, and has organized protests, including one at Delhi's Jantar Mantar. The party's website was taken down, and its social media presence has been affected, but it continues to attract attention and support.

sources.title: Why Youth Anger Alone Won't Sustain the CJP - Frontline
